title: Tangy Light Moroccan Yuzu-Kosho Couscous Royal
description: A comforting, lacto-vegetarian-friendly Moroccan dish, featuring tangy yuzu-kosho and crunchy nuka-pickle, all wrapped in a light and fluffy couscous. This recipe is perfect for a weeknight dinner or a special occasion.

ingredients.name: Yuzu Kosho
ingredients.notes: See notes for making yuzu kosho from scratch.

ingredients.name: Nuka Pickle (1 cup)
ingredients.notes: See notes for making nuka pickle from scratch.

instructions: In a medium saucepan, bring the water to a boil. Reduce heat to low and cover with a lid, cooking for 5 minutes or according to package instructions for couscous.
In a large skillet, heat the vegetable oil over medium-high heat. Add the chopped onion and cook until softened, 3-4 minutes.
Add the minced garlic to the skillet and cook for an additional minute.
Add the nuka pickle to the skillet and stir to combine with the onion and garlic mixture.
Stir in the yuzu kosho and cook for 1-2 minutes, until fragrant.
Add the cooked couscous to the skillet and stir to combine with the onion, garlic, and nuka pickle mixture.
Stir in the chopped cilantro, lemon juice, and salt.
Taste and adjust seasoning as needed.
Serve warm, garnished with additional cilantro if desired.
